question.text: Mr. Kapoor has invested Rs. 50 lakh in an equity fund and wants regular monthly income. His distributor suggests three options: SWP from Growth plan, IDCW Payout, and IDCW Re-investment. Which option provides the MOST tax-efficient regular income?

answer.text: SWP from the Growth option, as only the capital gains component of each withdrawal is taxed
IDCW Payout, because mutual fund dividends are tax-free
IDCW Re-investment, because the reinvested amount is not taxed
All three options have identical tax treatment

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** SWP from the Growth option is typically the most tax-efficient way to generate regular income. Each SWP withdrawal is a partial redemption — only the capital gains component (difference between redemption NAV and cost of acquisition) is taxed. The principal return portion is not taxed. In contrast, IDCW is taxed at the investor's slab rate on the entire distribution amount.
**The Trap:** IDCW in mutual funds is NOT tax-free (this changed from April 2020). The entire IDCW amount is added to the investor's income and taxed at their applicable slab rate. IDCW Re-investment is also taxed at the time of distribution even though the money stays in the fund. TDS is deducted if IDCW exceeds Rs. 5,000 per year.
**Cross-Exam Context:** After holding for more than 1 year, equity fund gains up to Rs. 1.25 lakh are tax-free (LTCG), and gains above that are taxed at 12.5%. This makes SWP from a long-held equity Growth fund extremely tax-efficient compared to IDCW which is fully taxable at slab rates.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** For clients needing regular income from equity investments, always recommend SWP from Growth option over IDCW. Show them the tax calculation comparison — the difference can be substantial, especially for investors in higher tax brackets. This advice demonstrates genuine value-addition.
